name: Maral Root
summary: It is best framed carefully because ecdysteroid narratives often outpace evidence quality.
description: It is best framed carefully because ecdysteroid narratives often outpace evidence quality.
contraindications: hormone-sensitive cancer such as estrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer because maral root shows estrogen receptor-binding activity
bleeding disorder or anticoagulant medication because maral root may affect bleeding risk
known allergy to Asteraceae family plants because Rhaponticum carthamoides is in the daisy family
immunosuppressant therapy because maral root may stimulate immune activity and blunt drug effectiveness
severe liver or kidney impairment because safety has not been established in these populations

sources.title: Chromatographic separation, determination and identification of ecdysteroids: Focus on Maral root (Rhaponticum carthamoides, Leuzea carthamoides)
Maral Root Extract and Its Main Constituent 20-Hydroxyecdysone Enhance Stress Resilience in Caenorhabditis elegans
Chemical Profiling and Biological Activity of Extracts from Nine Norwegian Medicinal and Aromatic Plants

dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Root/rhizome preparations are commonly used as teas and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
typical_dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Root/rhizome preparations are commonly used as teas and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
